summary: Focused client repair resolved the smaller-viewport first-screen UX evidence gap. The 1280x720 and 1440x900 screenshots now show player identity/situation/objective/recommended action, an actionable task rail summary, the map legend, and a visible Pixi map surface in the actual first viewport. This does not approve or reopen M7 content lock.

changes.summary: Kept the beta shell in a compressed two-column layout at 1280-class desktop widths, moved the map legend before the map surface, compacted first-screen orientation and task rail cards, preserved task rail drawer visibility, and corrected low-contrast player guidance copy colors.

Upgraded UX recovery smoke from DOM-presence checks to viewport-intersection and visible-area assertions for orientation, task rail cards, map surface, and map legend at 1440x900 and 1280x720; retained guidance and decision-assistant behavior checks.

tests_run.result: Modified files are limited to apps/web/src/styles/shell.css, tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts, the two regenerated screenshot evidence PNGs, plus this client handoff.
Listed active task registry; M7-UX-RECOVERY-VALIDATION-001 remains IN_PROGRESS.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-UX-RECOVERY-VALIDATION-001
pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts -g "M7 task rail is first-screen actionable|M7 UX recovery smoke" --project=chromium
tests_run.result: 2 passed; regenerated the 1280x720 and 1440x900 evidence screenshots.
Vite web build completed successfully with the existing non-blocking large chunk warning.
Storybook build completed successfully with non-blocking Vite chunk-size and plugin timing warnings.
Playwright Chromium suite passed: 26 tests, 26 passed.
Initial run failed at format:check for tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts after the E2E edit.
Format, lint, typecheck, and architecture checks passed.

decisions.decision: Do not stack the beta shell at 1280-class desktop widths; use a compressed two-column layout so the map and task rail share the first viewport.
Place the legend immediately before the map surface so route semantics are visible before the player reads the situation map.
This repair is validation support only and does not reopen or approve M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001.

risks: The 1280x720 layout is intentionally dense. It now proves the required first-screen elements, but further UX polish may still improve comfort at high text scaling.
Task rail cards hide some secondary explanatory copy at 1280-class widths to preserve actionable summary and drawer visibility.
requested_action: test_engineer should review the repaired viewport-visible evidence and rerun/extend validation as needed; lead_orchestrator should keep formal content lock blocked until reviewer acceptance and subsequent content-lock reconsideration.
